good morning. I’ve encountered an inconsistency with the label—it doesn't match the product specifications. The label reads "utt truf bite sea salk," whereas the flow wrap and the spec sheet read "utt truf bite sea salt." I spoke with the guys at IMS, but they mentioned that they don't have the option to modify this.

Laura Brady - I've asked the team to go ahead and label these using the labels that the system provides. I expect the problem is just a type and we should still fix it, but it feels like a better idea to have them labeled now and then decide if we want to over-label later. Let me know if you feel differently.
We have never had anyone complain.  However, I can pout this on my list to correct later when people are out of the labelview file.  We would have to shut this down at all three buildings to fix.

Yeah, that's a much bigger fix. I think we're making the right call though. Candon Watt - is there anything we need to do (beyond documenting in safetychain) to be prepared in case a complaint does come back?
Nope that’s it
L3 closed out of the file and I was able to make the change to the label.  Anything they print from here on will be correct.
Thanks now it’s correct

Following up on what was discussed during shift change regarding the chiller being received turned off on Monday. I spoke with Estefanía and she confirmed that the line shutdown procedure currently states that the chiller should be turned off. However, she mentioned that for the past several months she has normally been receiving the line with the chiller already in ON. Third shift had previously informed her that it was being left in ON because the PTL temperatures would drop if the chiller was turned off. The important thing is to verify what the correct shutdown procedure should be moving forward. Should the chiller be turned off or left in ON? If needed, the shutdown procedure should be updated to reflect the correct process. Preston Elmer could you please clarify what the correct procedure should be?
Hopper pass ATP
Oil is now circulating in melting mode.

When I called Maintenance to help us install the rollers on Chicane belts, he noticed that we were missing one roller —since the third shift was the one that performed the disassembly. We have already searched for it, but we cannot find it.
If the chiller is turned off, we lose pressure in the PTL water. This will fault the heating loop and it will stop heating and can freeze everything from ptl tank to the sweco pipe.

Leave the chiller on. We run glycol in L3 and will not have water freezing issues, the PTL is using the same glycol loop, also different in L3. The chocolate will freeze in the PTL and the strainer filter if we don't leave it on.

Chad Stone Robert Villarreal Rich Brierley Preston Elmer During the cleaning of the buffer tank walls with the pump sprayer. The Brass cap/Fitting fell into the buffer tank. After consulting Robert he advised to start the process over and find the brass Fittings. I have the staff using a mesh screen over each buck to help find the Fitting.
Noze plate. Done

The tank will need to be drained to remove the nozzle tip before it gets into the pump or temper unit. 
Because it's metal, it will absolutely destroy anything it encounters in the temper unit. Best hope is it gets stuck in the pump and destroys that instead of the temper unit. 
For this reason I ask it be drained and stop pumping oil through the system. 
 
If the metal tip is found while emptying the tank out then circulation can continue as long as all of the tip is found intact

Nathan Topham Mary Montilla To report on observations made while the team lead was packing pouches into trays: it was noted that Matrix 5 is experiencing intermittent issues with date and lot printing. Specifically, the printout occasionally appears blurry or is missing entirely, before subsequently resuming correct printing. It is recommended that the equipment undergo a thorough inspection and continuous monitoring to prevent the release of products with coding or appearance defects.

0.36096 equals 163.728 grams.
 
163.728 divided by 1.88 average is 87.089 pieces per pouch 
 
Amy Hadrich check my math please

If we are targeting 95 pieces per pouch, that is an extra 8 pieces per pouch that we are using compared to the BOM.
 
Last production we produced 113,228 pouches.
 
That equals 905,824 pretzels or 3754lbs of pretzels adjusted out.

We did not receive the 2pm transfer for cereal, we will be requesting out of FIFO from L2 so we can continue running until the truck is here. Curt Willis

Daniel Bringas Curt Willis, doing the math to run out the cases as of now it seems we will be done 4 or 6 hours in third shift. 

The line was running under schedule rate 72 pouches a min but schedule says 81 pouches a min. 

Daniel Bringas the last run we did we were scheduled at 72 pouches a min did we mean schedule it for 81 pouches a min?
These are the differences in run rates.
81 ppm is the new standard for 3 Matrix Machines Amy Hadrich can you confirm, please?

The budget schematic is based off of 2 matrix for 60 ppm, there is not an official standard for 3 matrix. We are still establishing the 3 matrix rate. 
 
We have ran this 3 times this year and have an average of 63 ppm. The 81 ppm minute came from a conversation stating that would be the max possible for the machines. However, planning for that rate is not ideal since we are still dialing in small pouches with 3 matrix. 
 
For labor standards on 3 matrix I use 72 ppm at 90% efficiency = 65 ppm
